commit.message:
🎨 Palette: Add clear action to SearchInput (#226)

* 🎨 Palette: Add clear action to SearchInput

This change adds a "Clear" button to the `SearchInput` component that appears when the input is not empty.

Key improvements:
- Added `RiCloseLine` button to clear the input immediately.
- The clear action triggers the `onChange` callback immediately, bypassing the debounce delay for better responsiveness.
- Added `aria-label` for accessibility.
- Adjusted input padding to prevent text overlap with the clear button.
- Verified visually with Playwright and ensured the project builds correctly.

Co-authored-by: jakwakwa <20184974+jakwakwa@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

`handleClear` calls `onChange("")` immediately, but the existing debounced `useEffect` will also call `onChange(query)` after the debounce delay when `query` becomes empty. This guarantees a second `onChange("")` emission on every clear, which can cause duplicate work for consumers that trigger side effects directly in `onChange` (e.g., network requests). Consider canceling/skipping the pending debounced emission on clear (e.g., switch to a debounced callback you can `cancel()`, or guard the effect with a ref to ignore the next debounced update after a manual clear).

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🎨 Palette: Refine SearchInput clear button and remove log

- Improved clear button size and padding for better UX.
- Fixed non-functional transition class.
- Removed accidental dev_server.log file.

Co-authored-by: jakwakwa <20184974+jakwakwa@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jakwakwa <20184974+jakwakwa@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(serializer): add missing return after free on corrupted RDB buffer (#1843)

* fix(serializer): add missing return after free on corrupted RDB buffer

In RdbLoadDeletedNodes and RdbLoadDeletedEdges (v18, v19), a misaligned
buffer triggers rm_free() followed by ASSERT(false). In release builds
ASSERT compiles to a no-op, so execution falls through to use the freed
pointer (use-after-free) and free it again (double-free).

Add explicit return statements after rm_free() in all four error paths
so the functions exit immediately regardless of build configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(serializer): add unit tests for deleted-entity buffer validation

Tests misaligned and aligned buffers for RdbLoadDeletedNodes_v19 and
RdbLoadDeletedEdges_v19. The misaligned tests verify the early-return
path does not cause use-after-free or double-free. The aligned tests
verify the happy path correctly marks entities as deleted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(serializer): skip misaligned buffer tests in debug builds

In debug builds, ASSERT(false) intentionally crashes (NULL deref) before
the return statement is reached, so the misaligned buffer tests can only
exercise the early-return path in release builds. Wrap them with
#if !RG_DEBUG guards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(serializer): use RedisModule_Assert for malformed RDB buffers

Replace silent return with RedisModule_Log + RedisModule_Assert(false) to
notify Redis and abort on corrupted deleted-entity buffers. Also validates
buffer count matches expected count (not just alignment).

Extends fix to v17 decoder which had no validation in release builds.
Converts unit tests to fork-based death tests to verify abort behavior.

Addresses review comment: use assert+exit instead of silent return, handle
short read / malformed RDB and notify Redis.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): use _exit in mock assert to prevent ASAN hang after fork

The mock RedisModule__Assert in death tests was a no-op, letting
exit(1) from RedisModule_Assert run ASAN atexit handlers in the
forked child. This hangs because ASAN tries to clean up thread-local
state that does not exist after fork(). Using _exit(1) in the mock
bypasses atexit handlers entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* removed unit test

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Roi Lipman <roilipman@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Forms: Fix fatal error when field render value is an array (#48032)

* Fix fatal error in get_legacy_extra_values when field value is an array

Guard against array values being used as array keys in the
get_legacy_extra_values method. When a form field (e.g., email) receives
array POST data, get_render_value() returns an array which cannot be
used as a PHP array key, causing a TypeError fatal error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enforce string values for single-value field types

For field types that should always be strings (email, name, url,
subject, textarea, ip), take the first element when array POST data
is submitted. Also use first-element fallback in get_legacy_extra_values
as a defensive guard for any pre-existing array values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rewrite test to reproduce the fatal without test isolation dependency

Use direct Feedback_Field construction with array value for a url-type
field and v2 format storage, which works in isolation. The key is using
'submit' context — get_render_submit_value() returns the raw array,
unlike 'default' which implodes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add parameterized tests for all non-extra field types with array values

Test every field type in $non_extra_fields (email, name, url, subject,
textarea, ip) with array values across both submit and default contexts.
Also verify checkbox-multiple arrays are preserved in submit context
and imploded in default context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Strengthen test assertions to verify data correctness, not just no-crash

Assert that extra values contain expected field values (Comment text
field in parameterized tests, checkbox-multiple array in preservation
test), not just that the method returns an array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix changelog entry: add trailing period and broaden description to cover both fixed sites

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Automattic/jetpack/sessions/7b5b7414-2667-4535-9781-d5ef5ee74cac

Co-authored-by: enejb <115071+enejb@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add test for legacy v1 checkbox-multiple array preservation

Legacy v1 format stores checkbox-multiple as JSON arrays with field
type 'basic'. Verify the full array survives the round-trip and is
not flattened by the reset() guard in get_legacy_extra_values().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify fatal error fix to minimal array handling

The type-aware unwrap logic added in e29b191b1c was broader than
necessary. The minimal fix for the fatal is to handle arrays in
sanitization; preserve arrays for all field types and let downstream
code handle them.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: enejb <115071+enejb@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(agent): resolve PhasicGenerationContext race condition

- phasic.ts: stateRef pattern forces behaviorType='phasic' in
  GenerationContext.from() even when setState() silently fails
- codingAgent.ts: await behavior.onStart() to prevent state machine
  starting before initialization completes
- base.ts: remove dead fire-and-forget ensureTemplateDetails() call
  from synchronous getTemplateDetails() method

Root cause: initialState.behaviorType defaults to 'unknown'. If
AgentComponent.setState() swallows an error during PhasicCodingBehavior
initialize(), the state never gets 'phasic' written to it. The stateRef
guard in getOperationOptions() ensures we always create a
PhasicGenerationContext regardless of persisted state drift.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add links command — graph health analysis (#46)

New command: npx @anokye-labs/kbexplorer links

Analyzes the knowledge graph and reports:
- Orphan nodes (zero connections)
- Broken references (connections to non-existent nodes)
- Weak clusters (no cross-cluster edges)
- Missing cross-references (content mentions without edges)
- Coverage gaps (source files with no content node)

Tested against kbexplorer-template: found 27 orphans, 179 missing
cross-references, 0 broken refs, 0 weak clusters.

Supports --json flag for programmatic output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ZSH completion for kubectl unbounded-net (#31)

* fix: rebuild images with proper version tag in release workflow

Replace imagetools re-tagging with actual Docker builds using the
release version tag. The CI layer cache means only the final VERSION
layer changes (~1s per image). Also builds plugins with the release
version ldflags instead of downloading from the CI run (which had
dev versions).

The release workflow now has:
- version: detect release merge, extract version from CHANGELOG
- build (matrix): rebuild images per-arch with proper VERSION arg
- manifest: create multi-arch manifests from per-arch images
- release: tag, render manifests, build plugins, SBOM, draft release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: ZSH completion for kubectl unbounded-net

The completion script referenced the old 'unbounded cni' command name,
causing completions to fail in ZSH. Updated:
- Root command Use: 'unbounded cni' -> 'unbounded-net'
- ZSH compdef targets to match 'unbounded-net'
- requestComp to use 'kubectl unbounded-net' for subcommand dispatch
- Usage display helpers to use 'kubectl unbounded-net'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: hardcode GOEXPERIMENT=nosystemcrypto in CI plugin builds

The 'go version | grep microsoft' detection doesn't work because
Microsoft Go's version string doesn't contain 'microsoft'. CI always
uses Microsoft Go (via go-download-base-url), so hardcode the
experiment in workflows. Makefile detection updated to check for the
systemcrypto_nocgo_linux.go file in GOROOT instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: rename Build/Test workflow to CI

Also update the release.yml workflow_run trigger to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: unbounded-net

commit.message:
tui: render /statusline configured items in bottom bar right side

Previously the bottom bar always showed only 'thread <id>' on the right
regardless of the /statusline configuration. The items were saved but
never rendered.

Changes:
- Add statusLineItemValue() to statusline.go: compact value renderer for
  each supported item (model, workspace, profile, trust, approval, thread,
  messages, theme, personality, fast).
- Add renderStatusLineBar() to statusline.go: joins configured items with
  '  •  ', skipping empty values (e.g. fast=off is hidden).
- renderStatusPane() now calls renderStatusLineBar() for the right side
  instead of hard-coding 'thread <id>' (thread is already a default item).

tui: fix ask dialog single Esc to cancel pending input prompt

Previously Esc in the ask/input dialog was routed to handleEsc() which
requires a double press within 500ms. A single Esc had no visible effect.

Now a single Esc while the ask overlay is active:
- Calls cancelRunFn() to cancel the agent run context, unblocking the
  goroutine that is waiting on replyCh (via ctx.Done() in userio.go)
- Clears pendAsk/askForm state and refreshes the viewport
- Appends a system message 'Input cancelled.'

[RCCL] Fix netOverride being skipped when rail trees are enabled (restore desired NIC mapping for targeted 4-NIC systems) (#4726)

## Motivation

This PR resolves a performance issue where enabling rail-optimized trees
caused an early return, skipping the netOverride logic on affected
systems.

On some 4-NIC MI3xx systems, this manifested as:

- enabling rail trees appearing to regress ring performance
- inconsistent behavior depending on whether rail trees were selected

`netOverride` is required for pairing GPUs with no PXB path to NICs on
these systems.

## Technical Details

1. Remove early return after treeRail success
2. Refactor override into helper `applyNetOverride(system,
romeTopoModels[i].options);`
3. Apply override after graph construction
4. Now runs regardless of what ring/tree are matched
5. Add runtime control `RCCL_DISABLE_NET_OVERRIDE` for debugging issues
with netOverride due to hard assumptions about system layout

## JIRA ID
ROCM-3200

## Test Plan

<!-- Explain any relevant testing done to verify this PR. -->

## Test Result

<!-- Briefly summarize test outcomes. -->

## Submission Checklist

- [ ] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

---------

feat: Wire genesis and world changes into core gameplay loop (#96)

* docs: add OpenRouter smart router integration guide

- Document use of openrouter-smart-router package
- Map task types to TTA pipeline stages
- Add examples for LLM integration in agents

* feat: wire genesis and world changes into game lifecycle

Connect existing genesis infrastructure to game creation and turn
processing. Three critical gaps fixed:

1. Genesis now runs during create_game() — template selection by
   preferences or world_id, WorldSeed construction, run_genesis_lite()
   call. Results stored in world_seed JSONB, narrative_intro returned
   to client.

2. World state updates now applied after turns — _dispatch_pipeline()
   translates LLM world_state_updates to WorldChange models via
   _ATTRIBUTE_TYPE_MAP and calls apply_changes().

3. Graceful degradation — all genesis/world-change calls wrapped in
   try/except. If Neo4j or LLM unavailable, game proceeds without
   world enrichment.

New: TemplateRegistry.select_by_preferences() resolves the circular
dependency (WorldSeed needs template, but template selection needed
WorldSeed). _ATTRIBUTE_TYPE_MAP maps 15 attribute keywords to 9
WorldChangeType values, sorted by descending key length to resolve
substring conflicts.

Tests: 26 new genesis integration tests (4 classes), all passing.
Quality: ruff clean, pyright 0 errors, 1342 tests pass.

[development-pm-c361] Complete TASK-184: Launch planning strategy

Created comprehensive go-to-market launch plan for Phase 2 visual canvas release:

Launch Channels:
- Product Hunt (page setup, hunter outreach, launch day schedule)
- Hacker News (timing strategy, post format, engagement plan)
- Social media (Twitter/X 10-tweet thread, LinkedIn, Reddit 5 subreddits)
- Email outreach (warm network, TypeScript influencers, cold leads)
- Community (Discord, GitHub, developer relations)
- Press (dev/AI publications, press release template)

Deliverables:
- Master timeline (pre-launch week, launch day, 4-week growth)
- Content templates (PH First Comment, HN post, email templates)
- Success metrics (Week 1: 500+ stars, 200+ downloads; Week 4: 2K+ stars)
- Risk mitigation (technical issues, negative feedback, competitive response)
- Launch checklist (40+ action items across product, marketing, community)

Target: Launch Tuesday 12:01am PST after TASK-185 QA completion.

commit.message:
Add structured logging to ConversationGrain and OllamaHttpClient

ConversationGrain:
- Log conversation initialization (new and already-initialized)
- Log model overrides on send
- Log Ollama request (model, message count) and response latency
- Log errors with context before re-throwing
- Log GetHistory on uninitialized grain

OllamaHttpClient:
- Log outgoing requests (model, message count, base address)
- Log Ollama response sizes and model counts
- Log warnings on HTTP errors and connection failures

commit.message:
Implement core PinePal features

- BLE device discovery and connection with exponential backoff reconnection
- Dashboard with live battery, heart rate, and step count from InfiniTime
- Step history bar chart with SQLite persistence (7d/30d/all time)
- Desktop notification forwarding to watch via D-Bus monitoring
- NavigationView-based UI with devices and dashboard pages
- GSettings for auto-connect address and notification toggle
- Flatpak manifest with Bluetooth and D-Bus permissions

Based on BLE protocol from watchmate by Andrii Zymohliad.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
